title: The Relational Model: Logic-Based Data Management
overview: An approach to managing data using a structure based on first-order predicate logic, where all data is represented in terms of tuples, grouped into relations.
historical_context: Proposed by E.F. Codd in 1970 as a way to decouple data storage from physical implementation.
mental_model: Think of data as a set of mathematical facts (tables) that can be queried and combined using the rules of logic.
tradeoffs: Potential for "Impedance Mismatch" when mapping to object-oriented code.
legacy: The foundation of almost all modern database systems (SQL).
ai_assisted_discovery_missions: Analyze E.F. Codd’s 1970 paper and its impact on the industry.
Compare the Relational model with the earlier Hierarchical and Network models.
